channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-20
item.description:
Our open-source research community has been buzzing with activity lately, with notable progress made in both the Davidvandijcke/coarse and JamieCummins/regcheck projects. In Davidvandijcke/coarse, significant updates were made to the 'dev' branch, including localization of the confirmation email and review page, as well as a fix for deployment. Meanwhile, JamieCummins/regcheck saw improvements to the "qwen" feature and logging functionality, with efforts also focused on bug fixing. It's exciting to see these projects evolve and intersect, with the localization work in Davidvandijcke/coarse potentially informing internationalization efforts in other projects like JamieCummins/regcheck. Overall, our community is making great strides in advancing open-source research tools, and we look forward to seeing the continued impact of these projects. With these updates, we're one step closer to creating a more seamless and efficient research experience for all.

121 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck37 commits
coarse31 commits
docpluck30 commits
metacheck20 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: docpluck: 4 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with several commits made to it. The documentation was updated to reflect that "log v2.4.95" and "REQUEST_11" have been shipped, with "deferred follow-ups" including "effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat". Additionally, the documentation was updated to record the "cross-session skill-file commit coordination", which is now fulfilled. A "chore" commit was made to perform "prior-session housekeeping", including a "REQUEST_10 customer broadcast, daily-digest cron-retry lesson, deploy smoke-gate fix". The tables feature was also updated, with a commit to "flatten fields for non-clinical result tables" as part of "REQUEST_11" in version "v2.4.95".

4 commit(s) today:

  • c1fa68b [main] docs(todo): log v2.4.95 / REQUEST_11 shipped + deferred follow-ups (effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat) — Gilad Feldman
  • 370b89c [main] docs(handoff): record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination (now fulfilled in b2c6aa2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b2c6aa2 [main] chore(docs+deploy): prior-session housekeeping — REQUEST_10 customer broadcast, daily-digest cron-retry lesson, deploy smoke-gate fix — Gilad Feldman
  • bce4b7f [main] feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, presumably to make it easier for users to access or install the OIF extension from the Chrome Webstore. The commit does not mention any other changes or additions beyond the Chrome Webstore link. There were no changes to existing features or modules mentioned in the commit message, only the addition of the link.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
